items.title: Vet's chance discovery of first H5 bird flu variant case during beach walk

children.content: A walk along the beach at Esperance, on WA's south coast, with a friend turned into a significant discovery.

articles.title: Bird flu could be catastrophic for nation's vulnerable species, experts warn

children.content: The black swan, the Tasmanian devil and Australian sea lions are among the species at risk if a deadly strain of avian influenza takes hold in Australia, experts are warning.

image.altText: A close-up of a brown bird

articles.title: Why Australia's first H5 bird flu case is 'deeply concerning'

children.content: Over recent years avian influenza has made its way across the globe. Now Australia has its first case. Here's what we know.

items.title: Police speech stuns descendants gathered at site of outback mass killing

image.altText: A man smiles in the foreground with a black and white image of a large cross in the background

children.content: Ronnie's great-grandfather collected bones and teeth from an outback creek bed. Now police say they're 'not proud' of what occurred.

items.title: How Neale Daniher helped Bryan Cousins after 'huge jolt' of MND diagnosis

children.content: WA football great Bryan Cousins is taking inspiration from the late Neale Daniher in his fight with motor neurone disease, as he pays tribute to son Ben for his efforts in combating addiction.

image.altText: Close-up of a man with short grey hair

items.title: Rescuers cry as baby turtles embark on 1,400km journey

children.content: Two turtles are set to be flown 1,400 kilometres in a journey that could take them from Western Australia to Madagascar.

image.altText: Turtle

items.title: How two budding chefs went from regional Australia to national TV

children.content: From cooking for farmers during seeding or creating a meat box that "wasn't edible", these chefs from Western Australia are now showing off their skills on a national stage for this year's season of MasterChef Australia.

gallery.altText: A woman with long hair wears a button up and apron. Beside her is a male with a moustache also in apron.

items.title: Mother holds onto hope her son's killer will be found after remote WA murder

children.content: The grieving mother of Josh Warneke, who was found dead on the side of a road in WA's remote Kimberley, has faced a long battle for answers. But how likely is it the 21-year-old's killer will be brought to justice?

image.altText: Image of a photograph of Josh Warneke placed in front of a memorial in Broome, Western Australia.

items.title: 'Not about mass surveillance': WA Police first in country to use live facial recognition

children.content: WA Police are set to become the first in the country to trial live facial recognition of crowds, describing the technology as safer than standard CCTV cameras.

image.altText: A close-up shot of two live facial recognition cameras on top of a pole.

children.content: Even from the sidelines, the leadership of Mat Ryan and Jackson Irvine will likely be as important as ever as the Socceroos turn their attention to Paraguay.

items.title: WA government wants federal help with Collie's coal transition

children.content: The state government has already tipped $700 million into the transition of the WA town of Collie away from coal. Now, the Cook government wants tens of millions of federal dollars to finish the job.

image.altText: sEVERAL COAL WORKERS HOLDING BLACK SIGNS AND A WHITE BANNER PROTESTING THE COAL TRANSITION

items.title: Why is your phone going off? Don't be alarmed, it's a warning system test

children.content: This week, phones in some regional towns have received a warning text alert and emitted an alarm as part of national warning system testing. Next month, your phone could receive it too.

image.altText: A phone notification with the words 'Priority AusAlert'.

items.title: 'Miscarriage of justice' leads to retrial for parents jailed for starving daughter

children.content: The WA Court of Appeal has overturned the guilty verdicts of Perth parents who starved their daughter after ruling that the judge's conduct in the jury trial amounted to a "miscarriage of justice".

gallery.altText: A picture of a girl in a yellow and white tutu posing.

items.title: Al Jazeera cameraman killed in Israeli strike in Gaza

children.content: Gaza health officials say Israeli strikes have killed at least 11 people, including two children and an Al Jazeera cameraman, in the latest violence to rock the Palestinian territory despite a ceasefire.

image.altText: A man filing with a phone on a tripod, turning back to look at the camera

items.title: WA mum warns changes to NDIS could take choices away from her son

children.content: Annette Nicholas and her husband last year became disability support workers to share their "lived experience" of raising 15-year-old son Tre, who is intellectually disabled and also lives with autism.

image.altText: A mother and son walking in a park.

items.title: Government says possum bridges successful, but skeptics question data

children.content: The WA government has released night-vision images showing scores of native wildlife are using rope bridges and underpasses along a $1.46 billion highway in the South West region, but not everyone is convinced.

gallery.altText: A night vision image of two ringtail possums crawling across a rope bridge toward the camera.

items.title: Fuel price cuts extended through July, but discount drops by half

children.content: The federal government is winding back its discount to the fuel excise over the next month as the 32 cents per litre fuel discount will drop to 16 cents.

image.altText: Five red petrol pumps in a bowser; one with a red handle, then a blue, a green, a dark blue and a black handle.

items.title: Is 'baby brain' real? A neuroscientist explains

children.content: The fog new parents feel is real, but it seems to be driven less by motherhood rewiring the brain than by exhaustion, overload and the relentless demands of caring for a tiny human.

image.altText: A close up photo of a woman hugging and kissing a baby in a navy striped top

items.title: The quest for the truth behind Australian nurses' massacre in WWII

children.content: A relative of one of the 21 Australian nurses massacred by Japanese soldiers on a remote Indonesian beach in WWII wants the real story of what happened to be told.

items.title: Stokes shines in county cricket as England struggles against NZ

items.title: It's the winter solstice, but you can expect more cold, dark mornings

children.content: The June solstice is the shortest day of the year in Australia, but not the one with the latest sunrise or coldest weather. Here's why.

items.title: 'Tony Modra is awake': Ricciuto describes optimism for star's recovery

items.title: Melbourne woman suffers acute liver failure after using knock-off drug

children.content: Megan Hancocks bought a drug labelled as the peptide compound retatrutide in cash from a beautician. Within a week of her second dose, she was in acute liver failure.

image.altText: A woman lies in a hospital bed with her eyes closed.

items.title: Hantavirus ship passenger recounts 'huge shock' as quarantine nears end

children.content: The first Australian to speak publicly about being onboard the MV Hondius shares his experience of the deadly outbreak on the cruise ship, as he prepares to leave a Perth quarantine facility and return home to his family.
